title: Support, Announcements, and Service Status API
purpose: Implement the first parent-owned application-layer slice for support ticket creation and user-facing support reads, plus dedicated announcement and service-status read APIs that can serve the support page and other browser surfaces. This spec focuses on safe DTOs, support-ticket orchestration, attachment metadata and storage abstraction boundaries, and versioned read/write endpoints. It intentionally does not implement admin or agent dashboards, customer-visible threaded conversations beyond the scoped ticket detail surface, background workers, live file-upload transport, Discord flows, rewards writes, dashboard redesign, or any new database schema unless repo reality proves a tiny support fix is absolutely required.

context.summary: The implementation plan and roadmap now place support/announcements/service status immediately after the completed dashboard/launcher/billing aggregation workstream. Repo reality shows the support, announcement, and service-status tables and locked vocabularies already exist, and the dashboard slice introduced minimal read-only announcement and service-status repositories for page composition. What is still missing is the proper application-layer slice for support repositories, support service orchestration, safe schemas, versioned routers, and focused tests for the support page and related browser-facing APIs.
repo_reality_summary: `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` now points at `specs/support_announcements_service_status_api.json` as the active workstream after the completed dashboard/launcher/billing slice, and `specs/next_phase_spec_sequence.json` defines this roadmap item as support ticket creation, attachment metadata or storage abstraction, announcement reads, service status by product, and notification-feed support.

Tracked models and migrations already exist for `support_tickets`, `support_ticket_messages`, `support_ticket_attachments`, `announcements`, and `service_statuses` in `app/db/models/` and `alembic/versions/20260413_2055_pdb055_support_attachment_tables.py`, `20260413_2105_pdb050_support_tables.py`, and `20260413_2108_pdb060_announcement_status_tables.py`. This spec should treat the persistence substrate as present and avoid reopening schema work.

`app/db/vocabularies.py` already locks the relevant values for `SUPPORT_REQUEST_TYPE`, `SUPPORT_PRIORITY`, `SUPPORT_TICKET_STATUS`, `SUPPORT_MESSAGE_AUTHOR_TYPE`, `ATTACHMENT_SCAN_STATUS`, `ANNOUNCEMENT_SCOPE`, `ANNOUNCEMENT_SEVERITY`, and `SERVICE_STATUS`, so the Build Agent should reuse those constants instead of inventing new strings.

`app/db/repositories/announcement_repository.py` and `app/db/repositories/service_status_repository.py` already provide narrow read-only repository methods used by `app/services/dashboard_service.py` for the dashboard notification feed and system-status card. There is still no `support_repository.py`, no support service, and no dedicated announcement or service-status service layer yet.

`app/api/routers/v1/__init__.py` currently registers auth, profiles, addresses, communication preferences, rewards, dashboard, launcher, and billing routes only. There is no `/api/v1/support`, `/api/v1/announcements`, or `/api/v1/service-status` router registration yet.

`app/api/deps.py` already exposes the session guard pattern the new routes should follow: authenticated session, verified session, and normal authenticated session contexts. `app/api/routers/v1/auth.py` already computes `can_access_support` for active, pending_verification, and suspended users, which is consistent with the architecture rule that support remains available while many other actions stay gated.

`app/schemas/common.py` already provides `MutationSuccessResponse`, `ApiErrorResponse`, and cursor pagination primitives, but there are no support, announcement, or service-status DTO modules under `app/schemas/` yet. The future implementation must add explicit safe schemas rather than returning ORM rows.

`app/services/__init__.py` currently exports auth, settings, Pay, dashboard, launcher, billing, and rewards services only. There are no support-oriented builder functions or service exports, and `tests/unit/test_application_layer_foundation.py` locks the service-package and router-boundary conventions that the new work should follow.

Focused runtime tests already exist for router registration, API error contracts, and the dashboard feed's current use of announcement and service-status repositories, but there are no `test_support_api.py`, `test_support_service.py`, `test_announcement_api.py`, `test_service_status_api.py`, or support repository tests yet.

The current dashboard implementation already reads announcement and service-status data through minimal repositories, so this spec should either preserve those seams or refactor them carefully behind services without changing the existing dashboard contract unless a small shared-read-model improvement is justified.

global_guardrails: Do not expand scope beyond this spec.
Do not add dependencies unless John explicitly approves them.
Do not duplicate Pay commercial business rules in parent.
Do not store sensitive payment details in parent.
Do not build admin dashboards unless explicitly scoped.
Do not redesign stable frontend pages.
Do not make Discord linkage affect rewards or product access unless the spec explicitly says so.
Do not allow frontend APIs to directly award points/rewards/badges.
Do not return raw ORM objects from routers.
Do not mark incomplete items complete.
Append progress entries to the absolute end of `progress/progress.txt`.
Run `python -m compileall app tests alembic` before marking implementation items complete.
Run `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test` before marking implementation items complete.

Treat support, announcement, and service-status tables plus locked vocabularies as existing repo substrate; avoid database-model or Alembic changes unless a tiny support fix is clearly required by repo reality.
Keep support attachment handling metadata-first: database rows may store metadata and storage keys, but not raw file blobs or public unauthenticated file URLs.

Use the existing auth/session rules so support ticket creation remains available to authenticated users who are pending verification or suspended, while routes that should require normal verified access continue using the stricter guards.
Keep announcement and service-status reads user-facing and read-only in this spec; administrative publishing, assignment, moderation, or dashboard tooling is out of scope.
Use the shared standard error contract, mutation-success contract, and cursor pagination contract already established under `app/schemas/common.py`.

items.title: Define Safe DTO Contracts for Support, Announcements, and Service Status
items.description: Add explicit request and response schemas for support ticket creation, support ticket summary and detail reads, attachment metadata summaries, announcement list items, and service-status list items. Reuse the common mutation-success and cursor pagination contracts where appropriate, and keep every DTO limited to frontend-safe fields.
acceptance_criteria: Schema modules exist under `app/schemas/` for support, announcements, and service status, and are exported through `app/schemas/__init__.py` if that matches the current package pattern.

Support request DTOs lock the allowed user-facing fields for request type, related product, priority, subject, description, and attachment metadata references without exposing internal ORM details or raw storage paths beyond safe identifiers.
Announcement and service-status list responses are explicit safe DTOs suitable for browser rendering and do not expose internal publishing or moderation state not needed by the frontend.
Any growing list surfaces in this spec, such as user ticket lists or announcement feeds, use the established cursor-pagination contract instead of page-number pagination.
Tests lock the new schema contracts and confirm they align with the shared API conventions already enforced by the application-layer foundation.

items.title: Implement Support Repository and Service Orchestration
items.description: Add the persistence and service-layer slice for support ticket creation and user-facing support reads on top of the existing tables. The service should own ticket-code generation, default status and scan-state handling, estimated-response labeling or fallback behavior, attachment metadata validation, transaction boundaries, and safe response shaping without building an admin workflow.
acceptance_criteria: A focused support repository exists with targeted methods for creating tickets, attaching metadata records, and listing or reading tickets for one account while preserving the repository-only persistence boundary.

A support service exists that orchestrates ticket creation and any scoped read models through explicit DTOs, uses service-owned transactions, and raises domain-specific exceptions for not-found or invalid support operations.

Attachment handling in this spec remains metadata-first: the service records attachment metadata and storage-intent details through a storage abstraction boundary or placeholder seam, but does not store raw file blobs in normal app tables.
Support behavior reuses the locked support vocabularies for request type, priority, ticket status, author type, and scan status rather than inventing new string values.
Tests cover normal ticket creation, invalid attachment metadata or empty payload rejection, account-scoped reads, and at least one authorization or account-status rule relevant to support access.

items.title: Add Announcement and Service-Status Read Services
items.description: Promote announcements and service status from the current dashboard-only repository seams into explicit read services that can power the support page, shared site notices, and other browser-facing surfaces. Keep these services read-only and compatible with the existing dashboard aggregation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Announcement and service-status service modules exist and follow the current router-to-service-to-repository dependency direction.
Announcement reads support active-window filtering and optional product scoping using the existing repository or a safe focused refinement of it.
Service-status reads return the current product status per product using the locked `online`, `degraded`, `maintenance`, and `offline` vocabulary.
Any necessary dashboard reuse is handled through shared read logic or a careful service seam without breaking the existing dashboard DTO contract.
Tests cover normal list behavior and at least one filtering or fallback behavior for announcements or service status.

items.title: Expose Versioned Support, Announcement, and Service-Status Endpoints
items.description: Add versioned routers under `/api/v1` for support tickets, announcements, and service status. The support router should expose the scoped customer-side write or read endpoints needed for the support page, while announcement and service-status routers should expose read-only browser-safe endpoints using explicit DTOs and the established auth guards.
acceptance_criteria: New routers are registered in `app/api/routers/v1/__init__.py` and mounted under the existing `/api/v1` application router path.

Support routes use auth guards deliberately so support remains available to eligible pending-verification or suspended users where the architecture allows it, while announcement and service-status routes use the least-permissive guard that still matches the intended browser surface.
Routers depend on services and dependency helpers rather than importing repositories directly.
API tests and router-registration tests prove the new `/api/v1/support`, `/api/v1/announcements`, and `/api/v1/service-status` paths are mounted and return standard DTO or error-envelope responses.
No admin-only actions such as assignment, moderation, publish editing, or internal-note management are exposed in this spec.

items.title: Verify Support, Announcement, and Service-Status Slice Completion
items.description: Run the required verification suite once the new DTOs, repositories, services, routers, and focused tests for support, announcements, and service status are in place. Only mark this spec complete when the user-facing slice is implemented without broadening into admin dashboards, worker infrastructure, or schema churn.
acceptance_criteria: `python -m compileall app tests alembic` passes.
`docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test` passes.
Focused tests cover schema contracts, support service behavior, announcement or service-status reads, router registration, and standard error-shape behavior for the new endpoints.
Progress is appended at EOF, and only fully completed items are marked complete.

completion_definition: Relevant tests are added or updated for support DTOs, support-service behavior, announcement and service-status reads, route registration, and API responses.
No unrelated dirty files are introduced.
No incomplete item is marked complete.
